symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe dyspnoea / air-hunger after an aspiration event
symptoms.onset: 12–36 hours after a witnessed choking/aspiration event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute hypoxaemic respiratory failure temporally locked to a known lung insult is the defining ARDS timeline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Refractory hypoxaemia — home oximeter SpO2 84% not correcting with rest

loinc_ref.display: Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shunt-pattern hypoxaemia that does not correct with position/rest reflects alveolar flooding; patient-obtainable, so admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: No relief from upright positioning; exhaustion/respiratory fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of orthopnoea/positional relief argues against a purely cardiogenic mechanism and marks severity.
Cardiogenic oedema typically improves somewhat with sitting upright (orthopnoea relief); no relief here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central cyanosis (dusky lips) with new drowsiness/confusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central cyanosis + altered mentation indicates critical hypoxaemia with end-organ impact — peri-arrest.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior heart failure, no orthopnoea/PND, not on cardiac medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of any cardiac history lowers the prior probability of an acute cardiogenic cause (does not exclude a first presentation).

symptoms.symptom_name: Fever 38.1 with recent aspiration and antibiotic course

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever supports an infective/inflammatory driver of the lung injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry, non-productive cough without pink frothy sputum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic (though not universal) cardiogenic oedema can produce pink frothy sputum; a dry cough is more in keeping with an inflammatory/alveolar process.
A normal JVP indicates normal right-sided filling pressures, against a congestive cardiogenic cause. Sealed clinician finding — not obtainable in telehealth.
Normal filling pressures with florid alveolar oedema is the hallmark of a non-cardiogenic (permeability) mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent S3 gallop and no new murmur on cardiac auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An S3 would support ventricular volume overload; its absence argues against acute heart failure. Sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse bilateral coarse and fine crackles throughout all lung fields

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widespread alveolar crackles reflect diffuse alveolar flooding. Sealed clinician auscultation finding.
Crackles rather than stony dullness/absent breath sounds argue against a large effusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent peripheral pitting oedema; warm flushed extremities with bounding pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No peripheral congestion, and a warm vasodilated periphery, point away from congestive heart failure toward a systemic inflammatory state. Sealed clinician finding.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cardiogenic vs non-cardiogenic oedema is harder to separate; a normal BNP argues strongly against acute heart failure.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal BNP makes acute decompensated heart failure unlikely. Hospital investigation — not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest radiograph: bilateral patchy alveolar infiltrates with normal heart size

investigation_details.expected_result: bilateral patchy alveolar infiltrates, normal cardiothoracic ratio
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: ARDS pattern vs cardiogenic congestion cannot be confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral infiltrates with a normal heart size fit ARDS rather than cardiogenic congestion. Hospital imaging — not available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Flat JVP + no S3 + no peripheral oedema + normal BNP + normal heart size with bilateral infiltrates together separate non-cardiogenic from cardiogenic oedema — but every element is clinician/investigation-derived and sealed from the AI Doctor.
Refractory SpO2 84% plus central cyanosis and drowsiness is a telehealth-obtainable cluster that mandates immediate 000 regardless of aetiology.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer with high-flow oxygen and pre-hospital ABG/monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient needs oxygen and ventilatory support that only paramedics/hospital can deliver; transfer is the intervention, not an office investigation.
Confirms bilateral infiltrates and a normal heart size to characterise the oedema.
Objectively excludes a cardiogenic cause per the Berlin definition.
Quantifies the degree of hypoxaemia/shunt and guides ventilatory support.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical home diuretic 'fluid tablet' trial before transfer
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A diuretic treats cardiogenic overload, not permeability oedema; trying it at home wastes critical minutes and can harm — it must NOT substitute for or delay 000.
